title: Dark Rich Polish Boudin Noir Tartine
description: A rich and romantic Polish dish featuring boudin noir, served with a glassy jelly and a touch of binchotan charcoal, perfect for a flexitarian friendly dinner
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit
Slice the boudin noir into 1/2 inch thick rounds
Heat the olive oil in a skillet over medium heat
Add the chopped shallots to the skillet and cook until caramelized
Add the red wine reduction to the skillet and cook until reduced by half
Add the boudin noir slices to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes per side
Toast the baguette slices in the oven for 5-7 minutes
Spread a layer of apple cider jelly on each toasted baguette slice
Top each baguette slice with a slice of boudin noir, a sprinkle of binchotan charcoal powder, and a sprinkle of chopped fresh thyme
